data.title: Delta Airlines Flight Makes Emergency Return After Engine Malfunction in Brazil
data.description:
A Delta Airlines flight experienced an engine malfunction shortly after departing from Brazil, necessitating an emergency return. According to RTHK, the incident occurred on Sunday night local time, involving Delta Airlines flight number 104. The aircraft, carrying 272 passengers and 14 crew members, took off from São Paulo's airport and encountered a fault in its left engine. The plane safely returned and landed without any injuries reported.

Social media footage shows what is believed to be the affected aircraft, with flames visible on the left side during takeoff. Delta Airlines stated that the flight was originally bound for Atlanta, USA, and all passengers have been accommodated. The cause of the malfunction is currently under investigation.

data.title: UN Peacekeepers Killed Amid Escalating Conflict in Lebanon
data.description: Three United Nations peacekeeper troops have been killed in Lebanon over the past 24 hours, as fierce fighting continues between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah militants in the southern part of the country. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing violence that has intensified in recent days. The conflict, rooted in longstanding tensions between Israel and Hezbollah, has seen a surge in hostilities, drawing international concern. The UN peacekeepers were stationed in Lebanon as part of efforts to maintain stability in the region. Their deaths underscore the volatile situation and the challenges faced by international forces in conflict zones. The United Nations has called for restraint and urged all parties to prioritize peace and dialogue to prevent further escalation. The situation remains tense, with the potential for further casualties as clashes persist.

data.title: Square Automatically Enables Bitcoin Payments for U.S. Merchants
data.description: Square has begun automatically enabling Bitcoin payments for eligible U.S. sellers across millions of merchants starting today. According to NS3.AI, this move allows merchants to receive USD by default through background conversion, while sellers have the option to opt out or adjust their preferences. Previously, the feature was voluntary since its late 2025 rollout.

data.title: Egyptian Currency Hits Record Low Amid US-Israeli Tensions with Iran
data.description: Egypt's currency has reached a new record low, raising concerns about potential further escalation in the ongoing US-Israeli conflict with Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing apprehension in the region as geopolitical tensions continue to mount. The depreciation of the Egyptian pound reflects the broader economic uncertainties faced by the country amid these international conflicts. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation, as any further developments could have significant implications for the region's stability and economic outlook.

data.title: XRP Faces Critical Support Levels Amid Market Analysis
data.description: XRP was trading at $1.35 on Monday, according to TradingView data. Analysts noted a weekly Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 29 on March 2, alongside a Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) setup that they associate with previous cycle bottoms. According to NS3.AI, experts emphasized the importance of XRP maintaining its support zone between $1.27 and $1.30 to prevent a further decline toward $1.15. A breach below this support could potentially lead to a drop to $0.80.

data.title: UK Prime Minister Warns of Challenges in Reopening Strait of Hormuz
data.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has cautioned that reopening the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping will be challenging. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Starmer's remarks made ahead of a meeting aimed at ensuring the strait's accessibility following the resolution of the conflict in Iran. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical maritime passage, and its closure has significant implications for global trade and energy supplies. The UK government is actively engaged in diplomatic efforts to address the situation and facilitate the resumption of shipping activities in the region.

data.title: Bugscoin to Resume BGSC Buybacks After Returning to Profit
data.description: Bugscoin has announced plans to resume BGSC buybacks in the second quarter of this year following a return to profitability. According to NS3.AI, the foundation reported a $1.86 million operating loss in the fourth quarter of last year, attributed to a significant buyback in September. Moving forward, Bugscoin intends to transfer repurchased tokens from centralized exchanges to its wallet each quarter, ensuring transparency through on-chain disclosure.

data.title: Soybean Oil Prices Surge Amid Rising Crude Oil Costs Due to Iran Conflict
data.description: Soybean oil prices increased by up to 3.4% in Chicago, driven by escalating crude oil costs linked to the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the geopolitical tensions on the biofuel sector. The rise in crude oil prices has bolstered the demand for biofuels, contributing to the surge in soybean oil prices. This development underscores the interconnectedness of global energy markets and agricultural commodities.

data.title: Launch of Blockchain Leadership Fund to Support Digital Asset Policy Advancement
data.description: The Blockchain Leadership Fund was launched on Monday, backed by Anchorage Digital and Chainlink Labs, to support candidates who advocate for digital asset and blockchain policy. According to NS3.AI, this initiative comes amid ongoing debates among lawmakers regarding the structure of the crypto market and stablecoin regulations. Progress in the Senate has been slow as the November elections approach.

data.title: Saudi Arabia's Position on the Iran Conflict: Potential Involvement and Interests
data.description:
Saudi Arabia's stance on the ongoing conflict involving Iran has drawn significant attention. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the complexities surrounding Saudi Arabia's potential involvement and its strategic interests in the region. The kingdom's geopolitical and economic considerations play a crucial role in shaping its response to the situation.

Saudi Arabia has historically viewed Iran as a regional rival, with tensions often flaring over political and religious differences. The current conflict presents both challenges and opportunities for Saudi Arabia, as it seeks to balance its security concerns with its economic ambitions.

The kingdom's decision to engage or remain on the sidelines will likely be influenced by its desire to maintain stability in the region, protect its oil infrastructure, and uphold its leadership role in the Arab world. Additionally, Saudi Arabia's alliances with Western powers, particularly the United States, may also impact its approach to the conflict.

As the situation evolves, Saudi Arabia's actions will be closely monitored by global observers, given the potential implications for regional stability and international relations.

data.title: ECB Board Member Stournaras: Swift Response Needed if Inflation Expectations Deviate
data.description: European Central Bank (ECB) Board Member Yannis Stournaras emphasized the necessity for the ECB to act promptly should second-round effects or signs of deviation in inflation expectations arise. According to Jin10, Stournaras highlighted the importance of monitoring inflation dynamics closely to ensure stability in the eurozone. He noted that any significant shifts in inflation expectations could require immediate intervention by the ECB to maintain economic balance. Stournaras' comments underscore the ongoing vigilance required by central banks in managing inflationary pressures and ensuring economic stability.

data.title: The momentum of a systemic sell-off in US stocks may be waning, and the market is poised for a rebound.
data.description: The Kobeissi Letter, in an article published on the X platform, stated that the momentum of a systemic sell-off in US stocks may be weakening. Algorithm-driven Commodity Trading Advisors (CTAs) sold $85 billion worth of US stocks in the past 30 trading days, the largest 30-day sales volume since the 2020 pandemic, when CTAs sold $105 billion. In comparison, the March-April 2025 correction resulted in $80 billion in sales. Currently, CTAs hold $37 billion in short positions in US stocks, the third highest level since 2019. Meanwhile, Goldman Sachs estimates that CTAs will buy over the next month regardless of market conditions, suggesting a potential market rebound.

data.title: Trump confirmed that the United States is in talks with Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf.
data.description: In an interview with the New York Post on Monday, US President Trump said that Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Ghalibaf was one of the Iranian officials communicating with the US regarding ending the war. When asked if he could trust Ghalibaf in cooperating with Washington, Trump replied, "We'll see." He added that he would inform them of the details in about a week. On Sunday, Trump told the Financial Times that Ghalibaf had authorized oil tankers to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. Trump interpreted this move as Ghalibaf being willing to make concessions to reach an agreement to end the war. Ghalibaf denied having held any negotiations with the US, but experts say he is a hardliner and has the potential to reach an agreement. According to the Wall Street Journal, Ghalibaf is one of two Iranian officials temporarily removed from the US and Israel's "death list." (Jinshi)

data.title: Phoenix Global Plans $6 Billion Expansion in Argentina's Vaca Muerta Shale Patch
data.description: Phoenix Global's CEO announced the company's intention to invest $6 billion in expanding operations within Argentina's Vaca Muerta shale patch. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision follows Argentina's recent move to enhance investor incentives, specifically targeting oil drilling activities. The expansion aims to capitalize on the newly broadened incentives, which are expected to attract significant investment into the region. Vaca Muerta, known for its vast shale oil and gas reserves, has become a focal point for energy companies looking to increase production. Phoenix Global's investment underscores the growing interest in Argentina's energy sector, driven by favorable government policies and the potential for substantial returns. The company's strategic move is anticipated to boost local employment and contribute to the country's economic growth.

data.title: Passenger Ferry Sinks Off Indonesia, 27 Missing
data.description: A passenger ferry sank near North Maluku province in Indonesia, leaving at least 27 people missing. According to RTHK, the local search and rescue department reported that the ferry departed from Taliabu Island last Sunday amid adverse weather conditions. By early Monday morning, the captain informed the ship owner that the ferry had sunk, and the owner subsequently reported the incident to authorities later that morning. Authorities immediately launched a search and rescue operation. Some individuals on board managed to evacuate using lifeboats, but the location of these lifeboats remains unknown, and rescue efforts are ongoing.

data.title: Trump Discusses Iranian Official's Role in Ending Conflict
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump, in an interview with the New York Post on Monday, identified Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf as one of the officials communicating with the U.S. to end the ongoing conflict. According to BlockBeats, when asked about the trustworthiness of Ghalibaf in cooperating with Washington, Trump stated, "We will see." He added that more details would be shared in about a week.

On Sunday, Trump told the Financial Times that Ghalibaf had authorized the passage of oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, interpreting this as a sign of Ghalibaf's willingness to make concessions to reach a peace agreement. Ghalibaf, however, denied any negotiations with the U.S., though experts note he is a hardliner capable of reaching an agreement.

The Wall Street Journal reported that Ghalibaf is one of two Iranian officials temporarily removed from the "death list" by the U.S. and Israel.

data.title: E*TRADE Expected to Underwrite SpaceX IPO for Retail Investors, Sources Say
data.description: E*TRADE is reportedly set to underwrite SpaceX's initial public offering (IPO) for small retail investors, according to sources. This move could provide individual investors with access to one of the most anticipated IPOs in recent years. SpaceX, known for its groundbreaking advancements in space exploration and technology, has been privately held since its inception. The involvement of E*TRADE, a prominent online brokerage, suggests a strategic approach to broaden the investor base for SpaceX's public market debut. Details on the IPO timeline and valuation remain undisclosed.

data.title: Germany Warns EU Methane Rules Could Hinder LNG Imports Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: Germany has expressed concerns that the European Union's regulations aimed at reducing methane emissions from oil and gas imports could obstruct essential liquefied natural gas (LNG) purchases. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Germany's efforts to diversify its energy suppliers and mitigate the impact of the ongoing Middle East conflict on energy prices. The warning comes as Germany seeks to secure stable energy sources in the face of geopolitical tensions that threaten to disrupt supply chains and elevate costs. The EU's methane regulations are part of broader environmental initiatives, but Germany argues that these measures could inadvertently affect its energy security strategy.

data.title: Calumet Shares Surge 14% Following Price Target Increase
data.description: Calumet shares experienced a significant rise of up to 14% on Monday after H.C. Wainwright increased its price target from $33 to $60 and maintained a Buy rating. According to NS3.AI, the investment firm attributed this optimistic outlook to stronger renewable fuels pricing and increasing demand. Additionally, Calumet began the week with a strengthened balance sheet, having refinanced its debt.

data.title: UK Prime Minister Starmer Announces 35-Nation Alliance for De-escalation Efforts
data.description: UK Prime Minister Starmer has announced the formation of a coalition comprising 35 countries aimed at de-escalating global tensions. According to PANews, this alliance seeks to address and mitigate ongoing international conflicts through diplomatic and cooperative measures. The initiative underscores the importance of multilateral efforts in maintaining global peace and stability.
